hypothesis: BLIND-SPOT lessons will show 2x+ contamination rate vs cited lessons (uncited = unchallengeable = contamination persists)
finding: BLIND-SPOT contamination rate (1.4%) is not higher than non-BLIND-SPOT (1.7%). Ratio 0.9x, not 2x+. Contamination distributes uniformly across knowledge base. BLIND-SPOT contaminated lessons are all low-risk (structural/citation-only, 0 content-dependent). Null hypothesis not rejected.
note: Mechanism corrected: BLIND-SPOT = not cited by others (low outbound influence); contamination vector = citing a falsified lesson (orthogonal property). Uncitedness does not predict uncorrected contamination at N=961.
actual: FALSIFIED: BLIND-SPOT contamination rate 1.4% (2/138) vs non-BLIND-SPOT 1.7% (14/823). Ratio 0.9x, not 2x+. Both BLIND-SPOT contaminated are structural/citation-only type, 0 content-dependent.
outcome: Hypothesis falsified. Contamination distributes uniformly. Correction priority is content-dependence type, not BLIND-SPOT status.
